input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is Access Control Lists (ACLs)?
target_value: About Access Control Lists (ACLs) Access Control Lists (ACLs) let a network administrator permit or deny passage of traffic based on network addresses, protocols, service ports, and other packet attributes. ACLs are composed of one or more Access Control Entries (called ACEs). Each ACE defines a filter criteria and an action, either permit or deny. If the traffic matches the filter criteria, the specified action is taken. The permit action permits the traffic to continue through the switch. The deny action causes the traffic to be discarded (dropped). ACEs can also log or count matching traffic.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is About applying ACLs to VLANs?
target_value: About applying ACLs to VLANs: ACLs can be applied to VLANs only in the inbound (ingress) direction. Sequence numbering If no sequence number is specified, the software appends new ACEs to the end of the ACL with a sequence number equal to the highest ACE currently in the list plus 10. The sequence numbers may be resequenced using the access-list resequence command.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the access-list copy command do?
target_value: The access-list copy command copies an IPv4, IPv6, or MAC ACL to a new destination ACL or overwrites an existing ACL.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the access-list copy command?
target_value: The syntax of the access-list copy command is: access-list {ip|IPv6|mac} <ACL-NAME> copy <DESTINATION-ACL>

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the access-list resequence command do?
target_value: The access-list resequence command resequences the ACE sequence numbers in an ACL.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the access-list resequence command?
target_value: The syntax of the access-list resequence command is: access-list {ip|IPv6|mac} <ACL-NAME> resequence <STARTING-SEQUENCE-NUMBER> <INCREMENT>

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the access-list reset command?
target_value: The syntax of the access-list reset command is: access-list {all|ip <ACL-NAME>|IPv6 <ACL-NAME>|mac <ACL-NAME>} reset.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the apply access-list control-plane command do?
target_value: The apply access-list control-plane command applies an ACL to the specified VRF. The no form of this command removes application of the ACL from the specified VRF.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the apply access-list control-plane command?
target_value: The syntax of the apply access-list control-plane command is: apply access-list {ip|IPv6} <ACL-NAME> control-plane vrf <VRF-NAME>

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the apply access-list (to interface or LAG) command do?
target_value: The apply access-list (to interface or LAG) command applies an ACL to the interface (Individual front plane port) or Link Aggregation Group (LAG) identified by the current interface or LAG context. The no form of this command removes application of the ACL from the current interface or LAG identified by the current interface or LAG context.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the apply access-list (to interface or LAG) command?
target_value: The syntax of the apply access-list (to interface or LAG) command is: apply access-list {ip|IPv6|mac} <ACL-NAME> {in|out}.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the apply access-list (to interface VLAN) command do?
target_value: The apply access-list (to interface VLAN) command applies an ACL to the interface VLAN (or range of interface VLANs) identified by the current interface VLAN context. Using the apply access-list command on an interface VLAN interface with an already-applied ACL of the same direction and type will replace the currently-applied ACL. The no form of this command removes application of the ACL from the interface VLAN (or range of interface VLANs) identified by the current interface VLAN context.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the apply access-list (to interface VLAN) command?
target_value: The syntax of the apply access-list (to interface VLAN) command is: apply access-list {ip|IPv6} <ACL-NAME> {routed-in|routed-out}.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear access-list hitcounts control-plane command do?
target_value: The clear access-list hitcounts control-plane command clears the hit counts for ACLs applied to the specified Control Plane VRF. Reorders the sequence numbers in an address object group.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear access-list hitcounts control-plane command?
target_value: The syntax of the clear access-list hitcounts control-plane command is: clear access-list hitcounts [{ip|IPv6} <ACL-NAME>] control-plane vrf <VRF-NAME>

target_value: The syntax of the object-group {ip|IPv6} address <OBJECT-GROUP-NAME> reset command is: object-group {ip|IPv6} address <OBJECT-GROUP-NAME> reset.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show access-list hitcounts control-plane command do?
target_value: The show access-list hitcounts control-plane command shows the hit count of the number of times an ACL (applied to the Control Plane) has matched a packet for ACEs with the count keyword. For ACEs without the count keyword, a dash is shown in place of a hit count.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show access-list hitcounts control-plane command?
target_value: The syntax of the show access-list hitcounts control-plane command is: show access-list hitcounts [{ip|IPv6} <ACL-NAME>] control-plane vrf <VRF-NAME> [vsx-peer]

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show capacities command do?
target_value: The show capacities command shows system capacities and their values for all features or a specific feature.

target_value: The syntax of the show capacities-status command is: show capacities-status <FEATURE>

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the class copy command do?
target_value: The class copy command copies a IPv4, IPv6, or MAC class to a new destination class or overwrites an existing class. Copying a class copies all entries as well.

target_value: The syntax of the class copy command is: class {ip|IPv6|mac} <CLASS-NAME> copy <DESTINATION-CLASS>

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the policy command do?
target_value: The policy command creates or modifies classifier policy and policy entries. A policy is made up of one or more policy entries ordered and prioritized by sequence numbers. Each entry has an IPv4/IPv6/MAC class and zero or more policy actions associated with it. A policy must be applied using the apply command.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the policy command?
target_value: The syntax of the policy command is: policy <POLICY-NAME>

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the policy copy command do?
target_value: The policy copy command copies a policy to a new destination policy or overwrites an existing policy. Copying a policy copies all its entries as well.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the policy copy command?
target_value: The syntax of the policy copy command is: policy <POLICY-NAME> copy <DESTINATION-POLICY>

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the policy resequence command do?
target_value: The policy resequence command resequences numbering in a policy.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the policy resequence command?
target_value: The syntax of the policy resequence command is: policy <POLICY-NAME> resequence <STARTING-SEQ-NUM> <INCREMENT>

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the policy reset command do?
target_value: The policy reset command changes the user-specified policy configuration to match the active policy configuration. Use this command when a discrepancy exists between what the user configured and what is active and accepted by the system.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the policy reset command?
target_value: The syntax of the policy reset command is: policy <POLICY-NAME> reset.

target_value: Ingress Routed VLAN Policy with IPv6 classes: Ingress IPv4 and/or IPv6 Analytics Data Collection (ADC) 8320 and 8325: There are two lookups available on Egress for these features. Applying both IPv4 and IPv6 ACLs on Routed-Egress at the same time is not permitted. Egress features that use one lookup:

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is Egress Routed VLAN IPv6 ACL?
target_value: 8325 TCAM lookup sharing: TCAM lookups for the following features are shared between corresponding IPv4 and IPv6 features. For example, if three IPv4 features are used with three equivalent IPv6 features, only three TCAM lookups are consumed instead of six. This is assuming the total number of IPv4 and IPv6 entries in each feature are less than 768.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip local-proxy-arp command do?
target_value: The ip local-proxy-arp command enables local proxy ARP on the specified interface. Local proxy ARP is supported on Layer 3 physical interfaces and on VLAN interfaces. To enable local proxy ARP on an interface, routing must be enabled on that interface. The no form of this command disables local proxy ARP on the specified interface.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip local-proxy-arp command?

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd command do?
target_value: The bfd command enables BFD support on the switch. BFD is disabled by default. The no form of this command disables BFD and removes all related configuration settings. To disable BFD, but retain configuration settings, use the command bfd disable.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip bootp-gateway <IPv4-ADDR> command do?
target_value: The ip bootp-gateway <IPv4-ADDR> command configures a gateway address for the DHCP relay agent to use for DHCP requests. By default DHCP relay agent picks the lowest-numbered IP address on the interface. The no form of this command removes the gateway address. 364 AOS-CX 10.06 Command-Line Interface Guide.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip dns domain-list <DOMAIN-NAME> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command do?
target_value: The ip dns domain-list <DOMAIN-NAME> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command configures one or more domain names that are appended to the DNS request. The DNS client appends each name in succession until the DNS server replies. Domains can be either IPv4 or IPv6. By default, requests are forwarded on the default VRF. The no form of this command removes a domain from the list.

target_value: The ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ] command configures a domain name that is appended to the DNS request. The domain can be either IPv4 or IPv6. By default, requests are forwarded on the default VRF. If a domain list is defined with the command ip dns domain-list, the domain name defined with this command is ignored. The no form of this command removes the domain name.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp apply access-list <ACL-NAME> command do?
target_value: The ip igmp apply access-list <ACL-NAME> command configures the ACL on a particular interface to filter the IGMP join or leave packets based on rules set in the particular ACL name. The no form of this command unconfigures the rules set for the ACL. NOTE: This configuration will override the ACL associated with IGMP snooping on the corresponding L2 VLAN.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp apply access-list <ACL-NAME> command?

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp last-member-query-interval <INTERVAL-VALUE> command do?
target_value: The ip igmp last-member-query-interval <INTERVAL-VALUE> command configures an IGMP last member query interval value in seconds on an interface, depending on the command context you are in. The no form of this command sets the value to a default of 1 second on an interface. Chapter 36 IGMP commands 475.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp last-member-query-interval <INTERVAL-VALUE> command?
target_value: The syntax of the ip igmp last-member-query-interval <INTERVAL-VALUE> command is: no ip igmp last-member-query-interval <INTERVAL-VALUE>

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp querier command do?
target_value: The ip igmp querier command configures an IGMP querier on an interface, depending on the command context you are in. This functionality will allow an interface to join in the querier-election process. The no form of this command disables IGMP querier on an interface. Configures the interval between IGMP queries on an interface, depending on the command context you are in. The no form of this command sets the IGMP querier interval to the default value of 125 seconds on an interface.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp querier command?
target_value: The syntax of the ip igmp querier command is: ip igmp querier interval <INTERVAL-VALUE>

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp querier query-max-response-time <RESPONSE-TIME> command do?
target_value: The ip igmp querier query-max-response-time <RESPONSE-TIME> command configures the IGMP querier max response time value in seconds on an interface, depending on the command context you are in. The no form of this command sets the querier max response time value to the default of 10 seconds on an interface. Chapter 36 IGMP commands 477.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp querier query-max-response-time <RESPONSE-TIME> command?
target_value: The syntax of the ip igmp querier query-max-response-time <RESPONSE-TIME> command is: no ip igmp querier query-max-response-time <RESPONSE-TIME>

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp robustness <VALUE> command do?
target_value: The ip igmp robustness <VALUE> command configures IGMP robustness on an interface, depending on the command context. The robustness parameter allows tuning for the expected packet loss on a subnet. The no form of this command sets the robustness value to the default of 2 on an interface.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp robustness <VALUE> command?
target_value: The syntax of the ip igmp robustness <VALUE> command is: no ip igmp robustness <VALUE>

target_value: The ip igmp snooping apply access-list <ACL-NAME> command configures the ACL on a particular interface to filter the IGMP join or leave packets based on rules set in the particular ACL name. input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp snooping auto <PORT-LIST> command do?

The no form of this command removes auto mode ports for the VLAN. Chapter 37 IGMP Snooping commands 499.

target_value: The ip igmp snooping fastlearn <PORT-LIST> command enables the port to learn group information when receiving a topology change notification. By default, fast learn is not enabled on ports. The no form of this command disables fast learn on the specified ports.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp snooping fastlearn <PORT-LIST> command?

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp snooping fastleave <PORT-LIST> command do?

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the interface tunnel <EXISTING-TUNNEL-NUMBER> command do?
target_value: The interface tunnel <EXISTING-TUNNEL-NUMBER> command creates or updates an IP tunnel. After you enter the command, the firmware switches to the configuration context for the tunnel. If the specified tunnel exists, this command switches to the context for the tunnel. By default, all tunnels are automatically assigned to the default VRF when they are created. The no form of this command deletes an existing IP tunnel. Chapter 44 IP tunnels commands 567.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> command do?
target_value: The ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> command sets the local IP address of a GRE tunnel. This address identifies the tunnel interface for routing. It must be on the same subnet as the tunnel address assigned on the remote device. The no form of this command deletes the local IP address assigned to a GRE tunnel.

target_value: The router ripng <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command creates RIPng process if not already created and enters the router ripng <PROCESS-ID> context for the VRF mentioned. input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure Configuring CoPP?
target_value: To configure CoPP, follow the documented procedure: 1. Configure the default CoPP policy, edit an existing policy, or create a policy with the command copp- 2. Add, edit, or remove classes in the policy with the command class 3. If the policy is not the active policy on the switch, apply it with the command apply copp-policy 4. Review the CoPP policy configuration settings with the command show copp-policy. Commands shown in the procedure include: policy..

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is Management of rotated log files?
target_value: Rotated log files are compressed and stored locally in /var/log/, regardless of the remote host configuration. Rotated log files are stored with respective time extension to the granularity of hour in the format file1–YYYYMMDDHH.gz (for example, messages-2015080715.gz). Rotated log files are replaced when the number of old rotated log files exceeds three. The newly rotated log file replaces the oldest rotated log file.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you troubleshoot Troubleshooting?
target_value: To troubleshoot, follow the documented procedure: 1. Modify the ACL to allow the ping traffic 2. Unapply the ACL from egress (8400/8320/8325 switches) or Control Plane 3. Ping a destination which is not matched by the ACL. For example, if the ACL is blocking traffic based on. Commands shown in the procedure include: PING 100.1.2.10 (100.1.2.10) 100(128) bytes of data; ping: sendmsg: Operation not permitted; ping: sendmsg: Operation not permitted; ping: sendmsg: Operation not permitted; ping: sendmsg: Operation not permitted; ping: sendmsg: Operation not permitted.

slots.topic: Troubleshooting Web UI and REST API access issues

target_value: To troubleshoot Web UI and REST API access issues, follow the documented procedure: 1. Log out from one of the existing sessions 2. If the session cookie is lost and it is not possible to log out of the session, then wait for the session idle.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is About AOS-CX?
target_value: AOS-CX introduction AOS-CX is a new, modern, fully programmable operating system built using a database-centric design that ensures higher availability and dynamic software process changes for reduced downtime. In addition to robust hardware reliability, the AOS-CX operating system includes additional software elements not available with traditional systems, including:.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure Initial configuration?
target_value: Perform the initial configuration of a factory default switch using one of the following methods:.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure Configuring in-band management on a data port?

slots.feature: Configuring in-band management on a data port

target_value: To configure in-band management on a data port, follow the documented procedure: 1. Use an Ethernet cable to connect a data port to your network 2. Configure a layer 3 interface on the data port 3. Enable SSH support on the interface (on the default VRF) with the command ssh server vrf default 4. Enable the Web UI on the interface (on the default VRF) with the command https-server vrf 5. Log in to the CLI 6. Switch to config context and enable the Web UI on the management port VRF with the command 7. Start your web browser and enter the IP address of the management port in the address bar, 8. The Web UI starts and you are prompted to log in. Commands shown in the procedure include: https-server vrf mgmt..

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure Configuring the management interface?
target_value: To configure the management interface, follow the documented procedure: 1. Switch to the management interface context with the command interface mgmt 2. By default, the management interface on the management port is enabled. If it was disabled, reenable it 3. Use the command ip dhcp to configure the management interface to automatically obtain an address 4. SSH is enabled by default on the management VRF. If disabled, enable SSH with the command ssh.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip static command do?
target_value: ip static <IP-ADDR>/<MASK>.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show interface mgmt command do?

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ntp authentication command do?
target_value: The ntp authentication command enables support for authentication when communicating with an NTP server. The no form of this command disables authentication support.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ntp authentication command?
target_value: The syntax of the ntp authentication command is: ntp authentication.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ntp authentication-key command do?
target_value: The ntp authentication-key command defines an authentication key that is used to secure the exchange with an NTP time server. This command provides protection against accidentally synchronizing to a time source that is not trusted. The no form of this command removes an authentication key.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ntp authentication-key command?
target_value: The syntax of the ntp authentication-key command is: ntp authentication-key <KEY-ID> {md5 | sha1} <PASSWORD> [trusted]

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ntp disable command do?
target_value: The ntp disable command disables the NTP client on the switch. The NTP client is disabled by default.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ntp disable command?
target_value: The syntax of the ntp disable command is: ntp disable.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ntp enable command do?
target_value: The ntp enable command enables the NTP client on the switch to automatically adjust the local time and date on the switch. The NTP client is disabled by default. The no form of this command disables the NTP client.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ntp enable command?
target_value: The syntax of the ntp enable command is: ntp enable.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ntp server command do?
target_value: The ntp server command defines an NTP server to use for time synchronization, or updates the settings of an existing server with new values. Up to eight servers can be defined. The no form of this command removes a configured NTP server.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ntp server command?
target_value: The syntax of the ntp server command is: ntp server <IP-ADDR> [key <KEY-NUM>] [minpoll <MIN-NUM>] [maxpoll <MAX-NUM>][burst | iburst] [prefer] [version <VER-NUM>]

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ntp trusted-key command do?
target_value: The ntp trusted-key command sets a key as trusted. When NTP authentication is enabled, the switch only synchronizes with time servers that transmit packets containing a trusted key. The no form of this command removes the trusted designation from a key.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ntp trusted-key command?
target_value: The syntax of the ntp trusted-key command is: ntp trusted-key <KEY-ID>

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ntp vrf command do?
target_value: The ntp vrf command specifies the VRF on which the NTP client communicates with an NTP server. The switch cannot function as both NTP master and client on the same VRF.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ntp vrf command?
target_value: The syntax of the ntp vrf command is: ntp vrf <VRF-NAME>

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ntp associations command do?
target_value: The show ntp associations command shows the status of the connection to each NTP server. The following information is displayed for each server: • Tally code: The first character is the Tally code: ◦ ◦ ◦ (blank): No state information available (e.g. non-responding server) x: Out of tolerance (discarded by intersection algorithm).: Discarded by table overflow (not used).

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ntp associations command?
target_value: The syntax of the show ntp associations command is: show ntp associations [vsx-peer]

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ntp authentication-keys command?
target_value: The syntax of the show ntp authentication-keys command is: show ntp authentication-keys [vsx-peer]

target_value: The show ntp servers command shows all configured NTP servers.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ntp servers command?
target_value: The syntax of the show ntp servers command is: show ntp servers [vsx-peer]

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ntp statistics command?
target_value: The syntax of the show ntp statistics command is: show ntp statistics [vsx-peer]

target_value: The show ntp status command shows the status of NTP on the switch.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ntp status command?
target_value: The syntax of the show ntp status command is: show ntp status [vsx-peer]

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show profiles available command do?
target_value: The show profiles available command shows all available profiles for the 8320, 8325, and 8360.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show profiles available command?
target_value: The syntax of the show profiles available command is: show profiles available.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show profile current command do?
target_value: The show profile current command shows current profile for 8320 and 8325 switch series.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show profile current command?
target_value: The syntax of the show profile current command is: show profile current.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure Configuring a layer 2 interface?
target_value: To configure a layer 2 interface, follow the documented procedure: 1. Change to the interface configuration context for the interface with the command interface 2. By default, interfaces are layer 3. To create a layer 2 interface, disable routing with the command no 3. Set the interface MTU (maximum transmission unit) with the command mtu 4. Review interface configuration settings with the command show interface.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure Configuring a layer 3 interface?
target_value: To configure a layer 3 interface, follow the documented procedure: 1. Change to the interface configuration context for the interface with the command interface 2. Interfaces are layer 3 by default. If you previously set the interface to layer 2, then enable routing support 3. Assign an IPv4 address with the command ip address, or an IPv6 address with the command IPv6 4. If required, enable support for layer 3 counters with the command l3-counters 5. If required, set the IP MTU with the command ip mtu 6. Review interface configuration settings with the command show interface.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the default interface <INTERFACE-ID> command do?
target_value: Sets an interface (or a range of interfaces) to factory default values.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the default interface <INTERFACE-ID> command?
target_value: The syntax of the default interface <INTERFACE-ID> command is: default interface <INTERFACE-ID>

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the interface command do?
target_value: The interface command switches to the config-if context for a physical port. This is where you define the configuration settings for the logical interface associated with the physical port. 48 AOS-CX 10.06 Fundamentals Guide.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the interface command?
target_value: The syntax of the interface command is: interface <PORT-NUM>

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the interface loopback command do?
target_value: The interface loopback command creates a loopback interface and changes to the config-loopback-if context. Loopback interfaces are layer 3. The no form of this command deletes a loopback interface.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the interface loopback command?
target_value: The syntax of the interface loopback command is: interface loopback <ID>

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip address command do?
target_value: The ip address command sets an IPv4 address for the current layer 3 interface. The no form of this command removes the IPv4 address from the interface.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip address command?
target_value: The syntax of the ip address command is: ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [secondary]

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip mtu command do?
target_value: The ip mtu command sets the IP MTU (maximum transmission unit) for an interface. This defines the largest IP packet that can be sent or received by the interface. The no form of this command sets the IP MTU to the default value 1500.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip mtu command?
target_value: The syntax of the ip mtu command is: ip mtu <VALUE>

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 address command?
target_value: The syntax of the IPv6 address command is: IPv6 address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK>{eui64 | [tag <ID>]}.

target_value: The syntax of the show allow-unsupported-transceiver command is: show allow-unsupported-transceiver.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show interface dom command do?
target_value: The show interface dom command shows diagnostics information and alarm/warning flags for the optical transceivers (SFP, SFP+, QSFP+). This information is known as DOM (Digital Optical Monitoring). DOM information also consists of vendor determined thresholds which trigger high/low alarms and warning flags.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show IPv6 interface command do?

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the shutdown command do?
target_value: The shutdown command disables an interface. Interfaces are disabled by default when created. The no form of this command enables an interface.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the shutdown command?
target_value: The syntax of the shutdown command is: shutdown.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is VLANs?
target_value: VLANs are primarily used to provide network segmentation at layer 2. VLANs enable the grouping of users by logical function instead of physical location. They make managing bandwidth usage within networks possible by:.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure Creating and enabling a VLAN?
target_value: To create and enabling a VLAN, follow the documented procedure: 1. Switch to the configuration context with the command config 2. Create a new VLAN with the command vlan.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure Disabling a VLAN?
target_value: To disable a VLAN, follow the documented procedure: 1. Switch to configuration context with the command config 2. Switch to configuration context for the VLAN you want to disable with the command vlan 3. Disable the VLAN with the command shutdown.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is Assigning a VLAN to an interface?

slots.topic: Assigning a VLAN to an interface

target_value: Assigning a VLAN to an interface: To use a VLAN, it must be assigned to an interface on the switch. VLANs can only be assigned to non-routed (layer 2) interfaces. All interfaces are routed (layer 3) by default when created. Use the no routing command to disable routing on an interface.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show capacities svi-count command do?
target_value: The show capacities svi-count command shows the maximum number of SVIs supported by the switch.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show capacities svi-count command?
target_value: The syntax of the show capacities svi-count command is: show capacities svi-count.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show system internal-vlan-range command do?
target_value: The show system internal-vlan-range command shows the VLAN range reserved for internal use.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show system internal-vlan-range command?
target_value: The syntax of the show system internal-vlan-range command is: show system internal-vlan-range.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show vlan [<VLAN-ID>] [vsx-peer] command do?
target_value: The show vlan [<VLAN-ID>] [vsx-peer] command displays configuration information for all VLANs or a specific VLAN.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show vlan port command do?
target_value: The show vlan port command displays the VLANs configured for a specific layer 2 interface.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show vlan port command?
target_value: The syntax of the show vlan port command is: show vlan port <INTERFACE-ID> [vsx-peer]

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show vlan summary command do?
target_value: The show VLANsummary command displays a summary of the VLAN configuration on the switch.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show vlan summary command?
target_value: The syntax of the show VLANsummary command is: show VLANsummary [vsx-peer]

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show vlan translation command do?
target_value: The show vlan translation command shows a summary of all VLAN translations rules defined on the switch, or the rules defined for a specific interface.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show vlan translation command?
target_value: The syntax of the show vlan translation command is: show vlan translation [interface <INTERFACE-NAME>] [vsx-peer]

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no vlan access [<VLAN-ID>] command do?
target_value: Creates an access interface and assigns an VLAN ID to it. Only one VLAN ID can be assigned to each access interface.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no vlan translate <VLAN-1> <VLAN-2> command do?
target_value: The no vlan translate <VLAN-1> <VLAN-2> command defines a bidirectional VLAN translation rule that maps an external VLAN ID to an internal VLAN ID on a LAG or layer 2 interface. Applies to both incoming and outgoing traffic. The no form of this command removes an existing VLAN translation rule on the current interface. NOTE: VLAN translation and MVRP cannot be enabled on the same interface.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no vlan trunk allowed [<VLAN-LIST>] command do?
target_value: Assigns a VLAN ID to an trunk interface. Multiple VLAN IDs can be assigned to a trunk interface. These VLAN IDs define which VLAN traffic is allowed across the trunk interface.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no vlan trunk native [<VLAN-ID>] command do?
target_value: Assigns a native VLAN ID to a trunk interface. By default, VLAN ID 1 is assigned as the native VLAN ID for all trunk interfaces. VLANs can only be assigned to a non-routed (layer 2) interface or LAG interface. Only one VLAN ID can be assigned as the native VLAN.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no vlan trunk native <VLAN-ID> tag command do?
target_value: The no vlan trunk native <VLAN-ID> tag command enables tagging on a native VLAN. Only incoming packets that are tagged with the matching VLAN ID are accepted. Incoming packets that are untagged are dropped except for BPDUs. Egress packets are tagged. The no form of this command removes tagging on a native VLAN.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the voice command do?
target_value: The voice command configures a VLAN as a voice VLAN. The no form of this command removes voice configuration from a VLAN.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the voice command?
target_value: The syntax of the voice command is: voice.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is Configuration and firmware management?
target_value: Checkpoints A checkpoint is a snapshot of the running configuration of a switch and its relevant metadata during the time of creation. Checkpoints can be used to apply the switch configuration stored within a checkpoint whenever needed, such as to revert to a previous, clean configuration. Checkpoints can be applied to other switches of the same platform. A switch is able to store multiple checkpoints.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the checkpoint auto command do?
target_value: The checkpoint auto command starts auto checkpoint mode. In auto checkpoint mode, the switch temporarily saves the runtime configuration as a checkpoint only for the specified time lapse interval. Configuration changes must be saved before the interval expires, otherwise the runtime configuration is restored from the temporary checkpoint.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the checkpoint auto command?
target_value: The syntax of the checkpoint auto command is: checkpoint auto <TIME-LAPSE-INTERVAL>

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the checkpoint auto confirm command do?
target_value: The checkpoint auto confirm command signals to the switch to save the running configuration used during the auto checkpoint mode. This command also ends the auto checkpoint mode.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the checkpoint auto confirm command?
target_value: The syntax of the checkpoint auto confirm command is: checkpoint auto confirm.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the checkpoint diff command do?
target_value: The checkpoint diff command shows the difference in configuration between two configurations. Compare checkpoints, the running configuration, or the startup configuration.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the checkpoint diff command?
target_value: The syntax of the checkpoint diff command is: checkpoint diff {<CHECKPOINT-NAME1> | running-config | startup-config}.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the checkpoint post-configuration timeout command?
target_value: The syntax of the checkpoint post-configuration timeout command is: checkpoint post-configuration timeout <TIMEOUT>

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the checkpoint rename command do?
target_value: The checkpoint rename command renames an existing checkpoint.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the checkpoint rename command?
target_value: The syntax of the checkpoint rename command is: checkpoint rename <OLD-CHECKPOINT-NAME> <NEW-CHECKPOINT-NAME>

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the checkpoint rollback command do?
target_value: The checkpoint rollback command applies the configuration from a pre-existing checkpoint or the startup configuration to the running configuration.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the checkpoint rollback command?
target_value: The syntax of the checkpoint rollback command is: checkpoint rollback {<CHECKPOINT-NAME> | startup-config}.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <REMOTE-URL> command do?
target_value: The copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <REMOTE-URL> command copies a checkpoint configuration to a remote location as a file. The configuration is exported in checkpoint format, which includes switch configuration and relevant metadata.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <REMOTE-URL> command?
target_value: The syntax of the copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <REMOTE-URL> command is: copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <REMOTE-URL> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <STORAGE-URL> command do?
target_value: The copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <STORAGE-URL> command copies an existing checkpoint configuration to a USB drive. The file format is defined when the checkpoint was created.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <STORAGE-URL> command?
target_value: The syntax of the copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <STORAGE-URL> command is: copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <STORAGE-URL>

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the copy <REMOTE-URL> checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> command do?
target_value: The copy <REMOTE-URL> checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> command copies a remote configuration file to a checkpoint. The remote configuration file must be in checkpoint format.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy <REMOTE-URL> checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> command?
target_value: The syntax of the copy <REMOTE-URL> checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> command is: copy <REMOTE-URL> checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the copy startup-config running-config command do?
target_value: The copy startup-config running-config command copies the startup configuration to the running configuration.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy startup-config running-config command?
target_value: The syntax of the copy startup-config running-config command is: copy startup-config running-config.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the copy <STORAGE-URL> running-config command do?
target_value: copy <STORAGE-URL> {running-config | startup-config | checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME>}.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy <STORAGE-URL> running-config command?
target_value: The syntax of the copy <STORAGE-URL> running-config command is: copy <STORAGE-URL> {running-config | startup-config | checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME>}.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> command do?
target_value: The show checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> command shows the configuration of a checkpoint.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> command?
target_value: The syntax of the show checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> command is: show checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> [json]

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show checkpoint list command do?
target_value: The show checkpoint list command shows a list of saved checkpoints.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show checkpoint list command?
target_value: The syntax of the show checkpoint list command is: show checkpoint list {all | <START-DATE> <END-DATE>}.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the boot set-default command do?
target_value: The boot set-default command sets the default operating system image to use when the system is booted.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the boot set-default command?
target_value: The syntax of the boot set-default command is: boot set-default {primary | secondary}.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the boot system command do?
target_value: The boot system command reboots all modules on the switch. By default, the configured default operating system image is used. Optional parameters enable you to specify which system image to use for the reboot operation and for future reboot operations.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the boot system command?
target_value: The syntax of the boot system command is: boot system [primary | secondary | serviceos]

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show boot-history command do?
target_value: The show boot-history command shows boot information. When no parameters are specified, shows the most recent information about the boot operation, and the three previous boot operations for the active management module. input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is Aruba Central Integration?
target_value: Aruba Central Integration: The Aruba Central network management solution, a software-as-a-service subscription in the cloud, provides streamlined management of multiple network devices. Arub a OS-CX switches are able to talk to Aruba Central and utilize cloud-based management functionality. Cloud-based management functionality allows for the deployment of network devices at sites with no or few dedicated IT personnel (branch offices, retail stores, and so forth). Arub a OS-CX switches utilize secure communication protocols to connect to the Aruba Central cloud portal, and can coexist with corporate security standards, such as those mandating the use of firewalls. This feature provides: • Zero-touch provisioning.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the aruba-central command do?
target_value: The aruba-central command creates or enters the Aruba Central configuration context (config-aruba-central). Disables connection to Aruba Central server. When the connection is disabled, the switch does not attempt to connect to the Aruba Central server or fetch central location from any of the three sources (CLI/Aruba Activate/DHCP). It also disconnects any active connection to the Aruba Central server.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the aruba-central command?
target_value: The syntax of the aruba-central command is: aruba-central.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is DNS?
target_value: The Domain Name System (DNS) is the Internet protocol for mapping a hostname to its IP address. DNS allows users to enter more readily memorable and intuitive hostnames, rather than IP addresses, to identify devices connected to a network. It also allows a host to keep the same hostname even if it changes its IP address.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure Configuring the DNS client?
target_value: To configure the DNS client, follow the documented procedure: 1. Configure one or more DNS name servers with the command ip dns server 2. To resolve DNS requests by appending a domain name to the requests, either configure a single domain 3. To use static name resolution for certain hosts, associate an IP address to a host with the command ip 4. Review your DNS configuration settings with the command show ip dns. Commands shown in the procedure include: VRF Name: vrf_mgmt; VRF Name: vrf_default; VRF Name: mainvrf.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip dns domain-list command do?
target_value: The ip dns domain-list command configures one or more domain names that are appended to the DNS request. The DNS client appends each name in succession until the DNS server replies. Domains can be either IPv4 or IPv6. By default, requests are forwarded on the default VRF. The no form of this command removes a domain from the list.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip dns domain-list command?
target_value: The syntax of the ip dns domain-list command is: ip dns domain-list <DOMAIN-NAME> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip dns domain-name command do?
target_value: The ip dns domain-name command configures a domain name that is appended to the DNS request. The domain can be either IPv4 or IPv6. By default, requests are forwarded on the default VRF. If a domain list is defined with the command ip dns domain-list, the domain name defined with this command is ignored. The no form of this command removes the domain name.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip dns domain-name command?
target_value: The syntax of the ip dns domain-name command is: ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ]

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip dns host command do?
target_value: The ip dns host command associates a static IP address with a hostname. The DNS client returns this IP address instead of querying a DNS server for an IP address for the hostname. Up to six hosts can be defined. If no VRF is defined, the default VRF is used. The no form of this command removes a static IP address associated with a hostname.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip dns host command?
target_value: The syntax of the ip dns host command is: ip dns host <HOST-NAME> <IP-ADDR> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ]

target_value: The show ip dns command shows all DNS client configuration settings or the settings for a specific VRF.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip dns command?
target_value: The syntax of the show ip dns command is: show ip dns [vrf <VRF-NAME>] [vsx-peer]

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is DCBx?
target_value: DCBx is a discovery and capability exchange protocol to discover peers and negotiate Data Center Bridging configuration. DCBx is specified as part of IEEE 802.1Qaz-2011. DCBx uses LLDP as the underlying protocol for exchange of parameters with the peer. The DCBx parameters are exchanged as LLDP TLVs.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd min-receive-interval command do?
target_value: The bfd min-receive-interval command sets the minimum time interval between received BFD control packets on an interface. The no form of this command sets the minimum interval between received BFD control packets to the default value of 3000 milliseconds.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd min-receive-interval command?
target_value: The syntax of the bfd min-receive-interval command is: bfd min-receive-interval <INTERVAL>

target_value: The bfd min-transmit-interval command sets the minimum time interval between transmitted BFD control packets on an interface. The no form of this command sets the minimum interval between transmitted BFD control packets to the default value of 3000 ms.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd min-transmit-interval command?
target_value: The syntax of the bfd min-transmit-interval command is: bfd min-transmit-interval <INTERVAL>

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf bfd disable command do?
target_value: The ip ospf bfd disable command disables BFD for OSPFv2 on the current interface. This overrides the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces.

target_value: The syntax of the ip ospf bfd disable command is: ip ospf bfd disable.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip route bfd command do?
target_value: The ip route bfd command enables or disables BFD on the specified static route. To disable BFD, issue the command without the bfd option.

target_value: The IPv6 ospfv3 bfd disable command disables BFD on the current OSPFv3 interface. This overrides the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 bfd disable command?

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the neighbor fall-over bfd command do?
target_value: The neighbor fall-over bfd command enables BGP to register with BFD to receive fast peering session deactivation messages from BFD. The no form of this command disables BGP for BFD. NOTE: Currently, BFD is supported only with IPv4 neighbors. Support to enable BFD with IPv6 neighbors will be added in a future release.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id> command do?
target_value: The clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id> command removes the protection switching and triggers reversion both in revertive and non-revertive operation. This command will not change the configured revertive operation mode.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring command do?
target_value: The erps ring command this command creates an ERPS ring with a given ID. The no form of this command removes all the configurations of the ring, including instances.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring command?

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> <port0 port1> interface command do?
target_value: The erps ring <ringid> <port0 port1> interface command this command configures the ERPS ring member port. An L2 interface in the switch is associated to one of the two member ports of an ERPS ring. In case of an interconnection node, only port0 is applicable for the sub-ring. The no form of this command removes the association of the ring port to the L2 interface on the switch.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> description command do?
target_value: The erps ring <ringid> description command this command adds descriptive information to help administrators and operators understand the purpose of a ring. 1-64 printable ASCII characters are allowed.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> hold-off-interval command do?
target_value: The erps ring <ringid> hold-off-interval command specifies hold-off interval in units of 100 ms. If specified, a defect is not reported immediately. Instead, the hold-off timer is started. On expiration of the timer, if the defect still exists, it is reported to protection switching. The default value for hold-off timer is 0. The no form of this command removes the configured value of the hold-off interval and sets it to the default value of 0.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> meg-level command do?
target_value: The erps ring <ringid> meg-level command the R-APS messages transmitted by ERPS take the form of OAM PDUs as defined in G.8013. Each OAM PDU is transmitted at a specified level known as the Maintenance Entity Group (MEG) level. This command configures the level with which the ERPS packets must be transmitted. The no form of this command removes the configured MEG level and sets it to the default value of 7.

target_value: The erps ring <ringid> parent-ring command this command associates a sub-ring to a parent-ring and is required for the sub-ring to notify the parent- ring on change in topology. The no form of this command removes the parent ring identifier. 56 AOS-CX 10.06 High Availability Guide.

target_value: The erps ring <ringid> sub-ring command this command is to configure a sub-ring. If not specified, the ring is a major-ring. The no form of this command removes the sub-ring configuration of the ring and configures it to be a major-ring.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> tcn-propogation command do?
target_value: The erps ring <ringid> tcn-propogation command this command is to configure a sub-ring interconnection node to pass a topology change notification to the ring instance for the parent ring whenever the topology of the sub-ring changes. The parent ring instance performs a Forwarding Database (FDB) flush and sends a protocol message to ensure that other nodes on the parent ring also perform an FDB flush. The no form of this command disables topology change notifications.

target_value: The vrf attach command attaches the interface to the VRF with the name <VRF-NAME>. The command can be entered in several different command contexts. CAUTION: The no form of the command detaches the interface from the named VRF and will remove all configurations from the interface and attach the interface to the default VRF. A warning message is displayed that prompts you whether to proceed: All Layer 3 configurations associated with the VRF will be deleted. Continue (y/n)? .

For MD5 authentication, set the password using the following command. For command details, see ip. Commands shown in the procedure include: ip ospf authentication {message-digest | simple-text | null | keychain}; ip ospf authentication-key.; ip ospf authentication-key <PASSWORD>; ip ospf message-digest-key {Key ID} md5 {ciphertext | plaintext} <KEY>.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the area <AERA-ID> default-metric <COST> command do?
target_value: The area <AERA-ID> default-metric <COST> command sets the cost of the default route announced to NSSA or stub areas. The no form of this command resets the cost of the default route announced to NSSA or stub areas, to the default value of 1.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the area <AREA-ID> range <IP-PREFIX> type {inter-area | nssa} [no-advertise] command do?
target_value: The area <AREA-ID> range <IP-PREFIX> type {inter-area | nssa} [no-advertise] command summarizes the routes with the matching address or masks. This command only works for border routers. The no form of this command removes route summarization for the configured IPv4 prefix address on the ABR. When using the no form of the command with the no-advertise option, enables advertising this range to other areas.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the area <AREA-ID> virtual-link <ROUTER-ID> command do?
target_value: The area <AREA-ID> virtual-link <ROUTER-ID> command creates an OSPF virtual link with a remote ABR and enters the vlink context. The no form of this command deletes an OSPF virtual link with the specified router ID of the remote ABR. If no <ROUTER-ID> is specified, the no form of the command sets the virtual link to the default settings. Sets the OSPF virtual-link authentication type that will be used for authentication with the remote ABR.

target_value: The distance <DISTANCE> command defines an administrative distance for OSPF. Administrative distance is used as a criteria to select the best route when multiple routes are present from different routing protocols. The no form of this command sets the OSPF administrative distance to the default of 110. Enables the OSPF process, if disabled. By default the OSPF process is enabled.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf passive command do?
target_value: The ip ospf passive command configures the interface as an OSPF passive interface. With this setting, the interface participates in OSPF but does not send or receive packets on that interface. The no form of this command resets the interface as active. With this setting, the interface starts sending and receiving OSPF packets.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf passive command?
target_value: The syntax of the ip ospf passive command is: ip ospf passive.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf priority command do?
target_value: The ip ospf priority command sets the OSPF priority for the interface. The larger the numeric value of the priority, the higher the chances for it to become the designated router. Setting a priority of zero makes the router ineligible to become a designated router or back up designated router. The no form of this command sets the OSPF priority for the interface to the default of 1.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf priority command?

A key chain configures rotating keys for packet authenticating, reducing the risk of keys being compromised. The no form of this command deletes the key chain used for md5 authentication.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the reference-bandwidth <BANDWIDTH> command do?
target_value: The reference-bandwidth <BANDWIDTH> command sets the reference bandwidth for OSPFv2. If the OSPFv2 interface cost is not explicitly set. then the cost of all the OSPFv2 interfaces is recalculated based on the reference bandwidth and link speed of the interface. For VLAN interfaces the link speed value is taken as 1 Gbps (if the OSPFv2 interface cost is not explicitly set). The no form of this command sets the reference bandwidth for OSPF to the default of 100000 Mbps.

If no IP address is configured on any interfaces on the router, OSPF will not form an adjacency.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure Guidelines and limitations?
target_value: For Guidelines and limitations, the guide documents the following restrictions, notes, or considerations: • VRRP must be enabled at the global level using the router vrrp enable command. It must also be enabled at the interface level at which you configure VRRP. • A maximum of 8 IPv4 and 8 IPv6 VRRP groups are supported on an interface. • A maximum of 256 VRRP groups is supported on a router. The groups can be IPv4 or IPv6. • Supported virtual router identification (VRID) range is 1-255. A maximum of 8 VRIDs can be used to achieve a scale of 256 VRRP groups by using the same VRID across different interfaces. • Proxy-ARP and Active-Gateway are not supported in conjunction with VRRP.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is IRDP?
target_value: ICMP Router Discovery Protocol (IRDP), an extension of the ICMP, is independent of any routing protocol. It allows hosts to discover the IP addresses of neighboring routers that can act as default gateways to reach devices on other IP networks.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure Configuring IRDP?
target_value: To configure IRDP, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable IRDP on an interface with the command ip irdp 2. Set the maximum hold time with the command ip irdp holdtime 3. Set the maximum router advertisement interval with the command ip irdp maxadvertinterval 4. Set the minimum router advertisement interval with the command ip irdp minadvertinterval 5. Set the IRDP preference level with the command ip irdp preference 6. Review IRDP configuration settings with the command show ip irdp.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip irdp command do?
target_value: The ip irdp command enables IRDP on an interface and specifies the packet type that is used to send advertisements. By default, the packet type is set to multicast. IRDP is only supported on layer 3 interfaces. The no form of this command disables IRDP on an interface.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip irdp command?
target_value: The syntax of the ip irdp command is: ip irdp [broadcast | multicast]

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip irdp holdtime command do?
target_value: ip irdp holdtime <TIME>.

target_value: The syntax of the ip irdp holdtime command is: ip irdp holdtime <TIME>

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip irdp maxadvertinterval command do?
target_value: The ip irdp maxadvertinterval command specifies the maximum router advertisement interval.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip irdp maxadvertinterval command?
target_value: The syntax of the ip irdp maxadvertinterval command is: ip irdp maxadvertinterval <TIME>

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip irdp minadvertinterval command do?
target_value: The ip irdp minadvertinterval command specifies the minimum amount of time the switch waits between sending router advertisements. By default, this value is automatically set by the switch to be 75% of the value configured for maximum router advertisement interval. Use this command to override the automatically configured value.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip irdp minadvertinterval command?
target_value: The syntax of the ip irdp minadvertinterval command is: ip irdp minadvertinterval <TIME>

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip irdp preference command do?
target_value: The ip irdp preference command specifies the IRDP preference level. If a host receives multiple router advertisement messages from different routers, the host selects the router that sent the message with the highest preference as the default gateway.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip irdp preference command?
target_value: The syntax of the ip irdp preference command is: ip irdp preference <LEVEL>

target_value: The show ip irdp command displays IRDP configuration settings.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip irdp command?
target_value: The syntax of the show ip irdp command is: show ip irdp [vsx-peer]

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure IPv6 Router Advertisement?
target_value: IPV6 RA provides a method for local IPV6 hosts to automatically configure their own IP address (and other settings such as a preferred DNS server) based on information advertised by switches/routers operating on the network.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure Configuring IPv6 RA?
target_value: To configure IPv6 RA, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable transmission of IPv6 router advertisements with the command no IPv6 nd suppress-ra 2. Optionally, configure IPv6 unicast address prefixes with the command IPv6 nd prefix 3. Optionally, configure support for DNS name resolution with the commands IPv6 nd ra dns server.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 address <global-unicast-address> command do?
target_value: The IPv6 address <global-unicast-address> command sets a global unicast address on the interface. The no form of this command removes the global unicast address on the interface. NOTE: This command automatically creates an IPv6 link-local address on the interface. However, it does not add the IPv6 address link-local command to the running configuration. If you remove the IPv6 address, the link-local address is also removed. To maintain the link-local address, you must manually execute the IPv6 address link-local command.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 address <global-unicast-address> command?
target_value: The syntax of the IPv6 address <global-unicast-address> command is: IPv6 address <global-unicast-address>

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 address autoconfig command do?
target_value: ipv6 address autoconfig.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 address autoconfig command?
target_value: The syntax of the IPv6 address autoconfig command is: IPv6 address autoconfig.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 address link-local command do?
target_value: The IPv6 address link-local command enables IPv6 on the current interface. If no address is specified, an IPv6 link-local address is auto-generated for the interface. If an address is specified, auto-configuration is disabled and the specified address/mask is assigned to the interface. To disable IPv6 link-local on the interface, remove IPv6 address link-local, IPv6 address <global- IPv6-address>, and IPv6 address autoconfig from the interface. NOTE: This feature is not available on the management VRF.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 address link-local command?
target_value: The syntax of the IPv6 address link-local command is: IPv6 address link-local [<IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK>]

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 nd cache-limit command do?
target_value: The IPv6 nd cache-limit command configures the limit on the number of neighbor entries in the ND cache. The no form of this command sets the cache limit to the default value.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd cache-limit command?
target_value: The syntax of the IPv6 nd cache-limit command is: IPv6 nd cache-limit <CACHELIMIT>

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 nd dad attempts command do?
target_value: The IPv6 nd dad attempts command configures the number of neighbor solicitations to be sent when performing duplicate address detection (DAD) for a unicast address configured on an interface. The no form of this command sets the number of attempts to the default value.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd dad attempts command?
target_value: The syntax of the IPv6 nd dad attempts command is: IPv6 nd dad attempts <NUM-ATTEMPTS>

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 nd hop-limit command do?
target_value: The IPv6 nd hop-limit command configures the hop limit to be sent in RAs. The no form of this command resets the hop limit to 0. This reset eliminates the hop limit from the RAs that originate on the interface, so the host determines the hop limit.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd hop-limit command?
target_value: The syntax of the IPv6 nd hop-limit command is: IPv6 nd hop-limit <HOPLIMIT>

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 nd mtu command do?
target_value: The IPv6 nd mtu command configures the MTU size to be sent in the RA messages. The no form of this command sets hop limit to the default value.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd mtu command?
target_value: The syntax of the IPv6 nd mtu command is: IPv6 nd mtu <MTU-VALUE>

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 nd ns-interval command do?
target_value: The IPv6 nd ns-interval command configures the ND time between DAD neighbor solicitations sent for an unresolved destination, or between duplicate address detection neighbor solicitation requests. Increase this setting when neighbor solicitation retries or failures are occurring, or in a slow (WAN) network. The no form of this command sets the ns-interval to the default value.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd ns-interval command?
target_value: The syntax of the IPv6 nd ns-interval command is: IPv6 nd ns-interval <TIME>

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 nd prefix command do?
target_value: The IPv6 nd prefix command specifies prefixes for the routing switch to include in RAs transmitted on the interface. IPv6 hosts use the prefixes in RAs to autoconfigure themselves with global unicast addresses. The autoconfigured address of a host is composed of the advertised prefix and the interface identifier in the current link-local address of the host. By default, advertise, autoconfig, and onlink are set. The no form of this command removes the configuration on the interface.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd prefix command?
target_value: The syntax of the IPv6 nd prefix command is: IPv6 nd prefix <IPv6-ADDR>/<PREFIX-LEN>

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 nd ra dns search-list command do?
target_value: The IPv6 nd ra dns search-list command configures the DNS Search List (DNSSL) to include in Router Advertisements (RAs) transmitted on the interface. The no form of this command removes the DNS Search List from the RAs transmitted on the interface.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd ra dns search-list command?
target_value: The syntax of the IPv6 nd ra dns search-list command is: IPv6 nd ra dns search-list <DOMAIN-NAME> [lifetime <TIME>]

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 nd ra dns server command do?
target_value: The IPv6 nd ra dns server command configures the IPv6 address of a preferred Recursive DNS Server (RDNSS) to be included in Router Advertisements (RAs) transmitted on the interface. The no form of this command removes the configured DNS server from the RAs transmitted on the interface.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd ra dns server command?
target_value: The syntax of the IPv6 nd ra dns server command is: IPv6 nd ra dns server <IPv6-ADDR> [lifetime <TIME>]

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 nd ra lifetime command do?
target_value: The IPv6 nd ra lifetime command configures the lifetime, in seconds, for the routing switch to be used as a default router by hosts on the current interface. The no form of this command sets lifetime to the default of 1800 seconds.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd ra lifetime command?
target_value: The syntax of the IPv6 nd ra lifetime command is: IPv6 nd ra lifetime <TIME>

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 nd ra managed-config-flag command do?
target_value: The IPv6 nd ra managed-config-flag command controls the M fLAGsetting in RAs the router transmits on the current interface. Enable the M flag to indicate that hosts can obtain IP address through DHCPv6. The M flag is disabled by default. The no form of this command turns off (disables) the M flag.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd ra managed-config-flag command?
target_value: The syntax of the IPv6 nd ra managed-config-flag command is: IPv6 nd ra managed-config-flag.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 nd ra min-interval command do?
target_value: The IPv6 nd ra min-interval command configures the minimum interval between transmissions of IPv6 RAs on the interface. The interval between RA transmissions on an interface is a random value that changes every time an RA is sent. The interval is calculated to be a value between the current max-interval and min-interval settings. The no form of this command returns the setting to its default, provided the default value is less than the current max-interval setting.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd ra min-interval command?
target_value: The syntax of the IPv6 nd ra min-interval command is: IPv6 nd ra min-interval <TIME>

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 nd ra other-config-flag command do?
target_value: The IPv6 nd ra other-config-flag command controls the O-bit in RAs the router transmits on the current interface; but is ignored unless the M-bit is disabled in RAs. Configure to set the O-bit in RA messages for host to obtain network parameters through DHCPv6. The other-config-flag is disabled by default. For more information on configuring the M-bit, see IPv6 nd ra managed-config-flag. The no form of this command turns off (disables) the setting for this command in RAs.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd ra other-config-flag command?
target_value: The syntax of the IPv6 nd ra other-config-flag command is: IPv6 nd ra other-config-flag.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 nd ra reachable-time command do?
target_value: The IPv6 nd ra reachable-time command sets the amount of time that the interface considers a device to be reachable after receiving a reachability confirmation from the device. The no form of this command sets the reachable time to the default value of 0. (no limit).

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd ra reachable-time command?
target_value: The syntax of the IPv6 nd ra reachable-time command is: IPv6 nd ra reachable-time <TIME>

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 nd ra retrans-timer command do?
target_value: ipv6 nd ra retrans-timer <TIME>.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd ra retrans-timer command?
target_value: The syntax of the IPv6 nd ra retrans-timer command is: IPv6 nd ra retrans-timer <TIME>

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 nd router-preference command do?
target_value: The IPv6 nd router-preference command specifies the value that is set in the Default Router Preference (DRP) field of Router Advertisements (RAs) that the switch sends from an interface. An interface with a DRP value of high will be preferred by other devices on the network over interfaces with an RA value of medium or low. The no form of this command set the value to the default of medium.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd router-preference command?
target_value: The syntax of the IPv6 nd router-preference command is: IPv6 nd router-preference {high | medium | low}.

target_value: The IPv6 nd suppress-ra command configures suppression of IPv6 Router Advertisement transmissions on an interface.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the sflow command do?
target_value: The sflow command enables the sFlow agent. • • In the config context, this command enables the sFlow agent globally on all interfaces. In an config-if context, this command enables the sFlow agent on a specific interface. sFlow cannot be enabled on a member of a LAG, only on the LAG. The sFlow agent is disabled by default. The no form of this command disables the sFlow agent and deletes all sFlow configuration settings, either globally, or for a specific interface.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the sflow command?

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the sflow disable command do?
target_value: The sflow disable command disables the sFlow agent, but retains any existing sFlow configuration settings. The settings become active if the sFlow agent is re-enabled. Sets the sFlow header size in bytes. The no form of this command sets the header size to the default value of 128. Sets the maximum number of bytes that are sent in one sFlow datagram. The no form of this command sets maximum number of bytes to the default value of 1400.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the sflow disable command?
target_value: The syntax of the sflow disable command is: sflow disable.

input_text: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure Configuring a DHCPv4 server on a VRF?

slots.feature: Configuring a DHCPv4 server on a VRF

target_value: To configure a DHCPv4 server on a VRF, follow the documented procedure: 1. Assign the DHCPv4 server to a VRF with the command dhcp-server vrf. This switches to the DHCPv4 2. If you want the DHCPv4 server to be the sole authority for IP addresses on the VRF, enable authoritative 3. Define an address pool for the VRF with the command pool. This switches to the DHCPv4 server pool 4. Enable the DHCP server on the VRF with the command enable 5. Configure support for persistent external storage of DHCP settings with the command dhcp-server 6. View DHCPv4 server configuration settings with the command show dhcp-server all-vrfs.

target_value: The dhcpv6-server vrf VRF-NAME command configures the DHCPv6 server to support a VRF and changes to the config-dhcpv6-server context for that VRF. The no form of this command removes DHCPv6 server support on a VRF. Disables the DHCPv6 server on the current VRF. The DHCPv6 server is disabled by default when configured on a VRF.
